content.formatted_body: While trying to get some corner cases correct around scoping and shadowing I noticed that engine 262 disagrees with v8 and jsc when evaluating the following:

m.new_content.formatted_body:
While trying to get some corner cases correct around scoping and shadowing I noticed that engine 262 disagrees with v8 and jsc when evaluating the following

function h() {
    function f() { return 1; }
    var r = "";
    r += f();
    for (var i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
        r += f();
        function f() { return 0;}
    }
    r += f();
    return r;
}

While trying to get some corner cases correct around scoping and shadowing I noticed that engine 262 disagrees with v8 and jsc when evaluating the following

function h() {
    function f() { return 1; }
    var r = "";
    r += f();
    for (var i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
        r += f();
        function f() { return 0;}
    }
    r += f();
    return r;
}
h();

content.body: Engine 262 is giving '100000000001', while v8 and jsc are giving '100000000000'.
Normally I'd trust engine 262 to be correct, but I'm struggling to see why in this case.
is that some sloppy mode annex b function scoping nonsense perhaps?
like, put it all in strict mode, what do they all say?
Yeah, looks like it, in strict mode they all agree.
Guess I need to go through annex b and work out which bit of annex b is governing this, and order a shirt that says, "It's always fucking Annex B." :-)
* Guess I need to go through annex b and work out which bit is governing this, and order a shirt that says, "It's always fucking Annex B." :-)

m.new_content.body: Guess I need to go through annex b and work out which bit is governing this, and order a shirt that says, "It's always fucking Annex B." :-)

content.body: (engine262 likely never chose to implement that sloppy function annex b stuff)
I think I've found where this is all specified, and I guess I'll have to preserve our engine's current behaviour in non-strict mode.
